claudeMd:
# Teeter — Browser-Based Head-Tilt Ball Game You are building a complete browser-based game from scratch. The player tilts their head side-to-side to steer a ball along a suspended 3D track. The webcam detects head tilt but is never shown on screen. ## Project Structure Create these files: - `index.html` — Main page with importmaps, inline CSS, loading/error UI - `js/main.js` — Entry point: camera init, game loop, state management - `js/tracker.js` — MediaPipe FaceLandmarker head tilt detection - `js/physics.js` — Ball physics (gravity, momentum, edge detection, falling) - `js/renderer.js` — Three.js scene (track, ball, lighting, camera) - `Dockerfile` — nginx serving static files on port 8080 - `nginx.conf` — nginx config for SPA ## Libraries (CDN only, no npm install) ### Three.js v0.183.2 Use importmap in index.html: ```html ``` Import in JS files: `import * as THREE from 'three';` ### MediaPipe Tasks Vision v0.10.33 Load via dynamic import or script tag. Key setup: ```javascript import { FaceLandmarker, FilesetResolver } from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@mediapipe/tasks-vision@0.10.33/vision_bundle.mjs'; const vision = await FilesetResolver.forVisionTasks( 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@mediapipe/tasks-vision@0.10.33/wasm' ); const faceLandmarker = await FaceLandmarker.createFromOptions(vision, { baseOptions: { modelAssetPath: 'https://storage.googleapis.com/mediapipe-models/face_landmarker/face_landmarker/float16/latest/face_landmarker.task', delegate: 'GPU' }, runningMode: 'VIDEO', numFaces: 1 }); ``` ## Head Tilt Detection Use atan2 on face landmarks for roll angle: - Landmark index 33 = left eye outer corner - Landmark index 263 = right eye outer corner - `roll = Math.atan2(landmarks[263].y - landmarks[33].y, landmarks[263].x - landmarks[33].x)` - This gives head tilt in radians. Map to lateral force on ball. - Apply exponential moving average for smoothing: `smoothedTilt = smoothedTilt * 0.7 + rawTilt * 0.3` IMPORTANT: The video element must be created programmatically and NEVER added to the DOM or made visible. Create it with `document.createElement('video')`, set autoplay and playsinline, attach the stream, but do NOT append it to any container. ## Physics (Custom, no library) Ball state: `{ x, z, vx, vz, y, vy, onTrack, falling }` Each frame (dt = time delta in seconds, capped at 1/30): 1. If on track: - Lateral acceleration: `ax = gravity * Math.sin(tiltAngle) * sensitivity` - `vx += ax * dt` - `vx *= (1 - friction * dt)` (rolling friction) - `vz += forwardSpeed * dt` (gentle constant forward motion, or just keep vz constant) - `x += vx * dt`, `z += vz * dt` - Edge check: if `|x| > trackHalfWidth`, set falling = true 2. If falling: - `vy -= gravity * dt` (full gravity downward) - `y += vy * dt` - When `y < -10`, reset ball to start position after brief delay Tuning constants (adjust for feel): - `gravity = 9.8` - `sensitivity = 2.5` (head tilt to force multiplier) - `friction = 3.0` - `trackHalfWidth = 1.5` (track is 3 units wide) - `trackLength = 50` (track is 50 units long) - `forwardSpeed = 2.0` ## Three.js Scene - **Track**: `BoxGeometry(3, 0.2, 50)` with `MeshStandardMaterial({ color: 0x8B7355 })` (wood-like). Position at y=0, centered. - **Ball**: `SphereGeometry(0.3, 32, 32)` with `MeshStandardMaterial({ color: 0xff4444, metalness: 0.3, roughness: 0.4 })`. Positioned at (0, 0.4, -20) to start at one end. - **Lighting**: AmbientLight(0x404060, 0.6) + DirectionalLight(0xffffff, 1.0) from above-right - **Background**: `scene.background = new THREE.Color(0x87CEEB)` (sky blue), add `scene.fog = new THREE.Fog(0x87CEEB, 30, 80)` - **Camera**: PerspectiveCamera, positioned behind and above the ball, looking slightly down at it. Follow the ball along z-axis. - **Ball rotation**: Each frame, rotate ball mesh around x-axis proportional to vz and around z-axis proportional to vx to show rolling. - **Track tilt visual**: Rotate the track group slightly around z-axis to match tilt angle (clamped, subtle). - **Side rails/edges**: Optional thin lines or geometry along track edges so player can see the boundary. ## Game States 1. `loading` — Show "Loading..." text. Initialize MediaPipe and Three.js. 2. `permission` — Prompt shown asking to allow camera. If denied, show error message and stop. 3. `playing` — Active game loop. Ball rolls, head tracking active. 4. `falling` — Ball fell off. Show it dropping, then auto-reset to `playing` after 1.5 seconds. ## index.html Structure ```html Teeter
Loading...
``` ## Dockerfile Use nginx:alpine. Copy files to /usr/share/nginx/html. Expose 8080. Custom nginx.conf to listen on 8080 with correct MIME types for .mjs files. ```dockerfile FROM nginx:alpine COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf COPY index.html /usr/share/nginx/html/ COPY js/ /usr/share/nginx/html/js/ EXPOSE 8080 ``` nginx.conf must: - Listen on port 8080 - Serve from /usr/share/nginx/html - Set `application/javascript` for .js and .mjs files - Add CORS headers if needed for CDN resources (usually not needed) ## Gotchas 1. **MediaPipe CDN import**: The vision_bundle.mjs is an ES module. You must import FaceLandmarker and FilesetResolver from it. The WASM files are loaded separately via FilesetResolver. 2. **Video element timing**: The video must be playing and have sufficient dimensions before calling `detectForVideo()`. Wait for the `loadeddata` event. 3. **detectForVideo requires timestamp**: Pass `performance.now()` as the second argument to `detectForVideo(video, timestamp)`. 4. **importmap + module scripts**: The importmap must appear BEFORE any `

checks: A 3D scene is visible showing a long, narrow suspended track (platform) floating in space with a sky-blue background and fog effect creating depth
A red/colored ball is positioned on one end of the track, resting on its surface
A loading message is shown initially while MediaPipe and Three.js initialize, then transitions to the game view
The browser requests camera/webcam permissions via a browser-native dialog on first load
If camera permission is denied, a clear text message is displayed explaining that camera access is required and instructing to reload
The webcam video feed is NOT visible anywhere on the screen — only the 3D game scene (ball, track, sky) is rendered
When the player tilts their head left, the ball rolls to the left on the track; tilting right rolls it right — with responsive, low-latency feel
The ball exhibits rolling physics: it accelerates gradually on tilt, has momentum that carries it after the head straightens, and decelerates due to friction
When the ball reaches the edge of the track, it visibly falls downward off the track into the void below
After the ball falls, the game automatically resets within a couple seconds — ball returns to the starting position on the track
There are no menus, score displays, level selectors, or any UI elements beyond the game scene and the initial loading/permission overlay
